title: High Efficiency Transformation Protocol (C2987H)

Ideally, DNA for transformation should be purified and resuspended in water or TE. However, up to 10 µl of DNA directly from a ligation mix can be used with only a two-fold loss of transformation efficiency. materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"HqALf","text":"MATERIALS","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":null,"data":null},{"key":"gcfac","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":null},{"key":"1MPeX","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":null}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":446,"guid":"41016F53341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"SOC Outgrowth Medium - 100 ml","mol_weight":0,"sku":"B9020S","url":"https://www.neb.com/B9020","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":2,"name":"New England Biolabs","link":"https://www.neb.com/","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/2.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/2.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}},"1":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":349236,"guid":"424F55DB341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"NEB 5-alpha Competent E. coli cells","mol_weight":0,"sku":"C2987H","url":"https://www.neb.com/products/c2987-neb-5-alpha-competent-e-coli-high-efficiency#Product%20Information","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":2,"name":"New England Biolabs","link":"https://www.neb.com/","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/2.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/2.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: Thaw a tube of NEB 5-alpha Competent E. coli cells On ice for 00:10:00 . Cells are best thawed on ice and DNA added as soon as the last bit of ice in the tube disappears. Cells can also be thawed by hand, but warming above 0°C will decrease the transformation efficiency.
Add 1 µL - 5 µL containing 1 pg - 100 ng, plasmid DNA to the cell mixture.
Carefully flick the tube 4-5 times to mix cells and DNA. Do not vortex.

Place the mixture On ice for 00:30:00 . Do not mix. For maximum transformation efficiency, cells and DNA should be incubated together on ice for 30 minutes. Expect a 2-fold loss in transformation efficiency for every 10 minutes this step is shortened.

Heat shock at exactly 42 °C for exactly 00:00:30 . Do not mix. Both the temperature and the timing of the heat shock step are important and specific to the transformation volume and vessel. Using the transformation tube provided, 30 seconds at 42°C is optimal.
Place On ice for 00:05:00 . Do not mix.
Pipette 950 µL, room temperature SOC into the mixture.

Place at 37 °C for 01:00:00 , shaking vigorously ( 250 rpm, °C, ) or rotating. Outgrowth at 37°C for 1 hour is best for cell recovery and for expression of antibiotic resistance. Expect a 2-fold loss in transformation efficiency for every 15 minutes this step is shortened. SOC gives 2-fold higher transformation efficiency than LB medium; and incubation with shaking or rotating the tube gives 2-fold higher transformation efficiency than incubation without shaking.

Warm selection plates to 37 °C . Selection plates can be used warm or cold, wet or dry without significantly affecting the transformation efficiency. However, warm, dry plates are easier to spread and allow for the most rapid colony formation.
Mix the cells thoroughly by flicking the tube and inverting.
Perform several 10-fold serial dilutions in SOC.
Spread 50 µL - 100 µL of each dilution onto a selection plate.
Incubate Overnight at 37 °C . Alternatively, incubate at 30 °C for 24-36 hours or 25 °C for 48:00:00 .
creator: New England   Biolabs
